sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano are struggling, winless in their last three matches, with a heavy 4-0 loss to Villarreal highlighting defensive issues. Valencia, meanwhile, showed resilience with a recent 1-0 win over Levante UD but suffered a 4-0 defeat to Real Madrid earlier, indicating inconsistency. Both teams are desperate for points, sitting at 11th and 16th in the league standings.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, matches between Rayo Vallecano and Valencia have been tight, with a 1-1 draw in April 2025 and a 1-0 Rayo win in December 2024. Historically, their games often produce under 2.5 goals (5/5 in recent head-to-heads), suggesting a low-scoring affair is likely today.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano have a solid defensive record, conceding just 14 goals (4th fewest in the league), but their attack struggles, scoring only 12. Valencia mirror this offensive drought with 12 goals but have conceded more (21), ranking them lower in defensive stability. Rayo's higher possession (53.3%) and better tackling stats could give them a slight edge at home.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano will miss key midfielder Isi Palazón, potentially impacting their creativity, but Jorge de Frutos (4 goals) remains a threat. Valencia have no major absences and rely on Hugo Duro (4 goals) for firepower. Stole Dimitrievski's recent form in goal for Valencia could be crucial.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (40% home win, 33% draw, 27% away win) and Rayo’s home advantage at Estadio de Vallecas, they are slight favorites. Valencia’s inconsistent form and poor away record tilt the balance towards Rayo, though a draw remains a strong possibility given recent head-to-head trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Rayo Vallecano take a deserved lead against Valencia with a 1-0 scoreline at Estadio de Vallecas. Rayo Vallecano dominated possession and created numerous scoring opportunities, culminating in a headed goal by Nobel Mendy in the 37th minute. Their attacking intent was evident through consistent pressure in the final third and multiple entries into the penalty area. Valencia, on the other hand, struggled to create meaningful chances, managing only a single shot on target and showing little threat in the attacking zones. The home side's superior aerial dominance and duel-winning ability further underlined their control of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano showcased strong attacking momentum for the majority of the first half, with frequent spells of sustained pressure in the opposition half. Their intent was particularly noticeable in the early stages and around the time of their goal, where they consistently pushed forward. Valencia had brief moments of momentum, especially in the middle of the half and towards the end, but these spells were short-lived and lacked the intensity to trouble the home defense. Overall, the attacking impetus clearly favored Rayo Vallecano, who rarely relinquished control.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: Nobel Mendy emerged as the standout player for Rayo Vallecano, not only scoring the decisive goal but also contributing defensively with clearances. His high rating reflects his all-around impact on the game. Players like Gerard Gumbau and Andrei Ratiu also played pivotal roles with their creative contributions and defensive solidity. For Valencia, Javier Guerra and Pepelu showed some promise in midfield, but the lack of support in the final third limited their influence. Hugo Duro, expected to lead the attack, remained largely ineffective in the first half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano's tactical setup allowed them to control the game through high possession and effective use of width, leading to multiple corner kicks and shots on target. Their strategy of dominating aerial duels paid dividends, especially with the headed goal. Valencia appeared disjointed, with a lack of cohesion between midfield and attack, resulting in minimal big chances created. Their low possession share indicates a defensive approach, but they failed to capitalize on counter-attacking opportunities. Adjustments will be crucial for them to turn the tide in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: Despite their dominance, Rayo Vallecano will be concerned about missed big chances, as converting these could have put the game out of reach already. Sharpening their finishing will be key to securing the result. Valencia needs to address their lack of attacking output by committing more players forward and improving their final third entries. Defensive organization will also be critical for them to avoid conceding again, as they were often exposed in aerial battles. Both teams have room to tweak their approach for the second half.
